modules.name: PostmortemsList
modules.description: Post-incident reviews and action item tracking

modules.name: ActionItemsTracker
modules.description: Track all corrective actions from incidents and postmortems

modules.name: AnalyticsPage
modules.description: Incident metrics, MTTR/MTTA trends, repeat incident analysis

events.name: incident:created
events.description: Broadcast to all responders and show ActiveIncidentBanner for SEV1/SEV2

events.name: incident:update_added
events.description: Real-time update in war room feed

events.name: incident:status_changed
events.description: Notify all responders and update status badge

events.name: incident:severity_changed
events.description: Alert if severity escalated to SEV1/SEV2

events.name: incident:responder_joined
events.description: Show in war room responder panel

flows.name: Declare Incident
flows.trigger: Responder or automated alert creates incident
steps: Auto-determine is_major based on severity (SEV1/SEV2) and customer_impact
If SEV1/SEV2 → immediately page on-call commander, broadcast to all responders
Commander acknowledges → set acknowledged_at, status = acknowledged
Responders join → open war room with live feed

flows.name: Resolve Incident
steps: Prompt to start postmortem if is_major = true
Commander closes when satisfied: status = closed, closed_at = NOW()

flows.name: Write Postmortem
flows.trigger: Incident is closed or commander starts postmortem
steps: Create Postmortem record linked to incident
Team collaborates on sections via real-time editor
Reviewer approves → status = published, notify stakeholders

computed_fields.name: mttr_minutes
computed_fields.formula: AVG(time_to_resolve_min) WHERE resolved_at IS NOT NULL AND period matches filter

computed_fields.name: mtta_minutes
computed_fields.formula: AVG(time_to_acknowledge_min) WHERE acknowledged_at IS NOT NULL

domain_rules: Only one commander per incident at a time; reassigning commander creates a system update entry
SEV1 incidents require a postmortem before the incident can be fully closed
Incidents cannot be deleted once they have any updates or responders
MTTR and MTTA calculated only on incidents with resolved/closed status
is_major auto-set on create/update; cannot be manually overridden
Postmortems are created at most once per incident (unique constraint)
Action items sourced from postmortem are tagged source=postmortem for tracking
ActiveIncidentBanner appears for all logged-in users when SEV1/SEV2 incident is open
generation_rules: War room page subscribes to all websocket events for the incident on mount; unsubscribes on unmount
ActiveIncidentBanner mounts at app root, subscribes to incident:created for SEV1/SEV2
LiveUpdatesFeed auto-scrolls to newest update; pause auto-scroll when user scrolls up
Postmortem editor uses TipTap with collaborative extension (websocket Y.js provider)
Incident timeline automatically inserts system updates on status changes (no manual entry required)
MTTR/MTTA stored on incident record after resolve to avoid recalculation on analytics queries
Page on-call integration: fire webhook to PagerDuty/OpsGenie on SEV1/SEV2 incident creation
Incident number auto-increments per org: INC-0001, INC-0002

org_id_injection.middleware: req.body.org_id is stripped; injected from req.user.org_id

conflict_response.message: Record was modified by another user. Refresh and retry.

concurrency.high_contention_tables: see entities with high write concurrency

audit_trail.triggers: application-level (before write hook) — never DB trigger to avoid log bloat

standard_constraints.name: chk_{table}_deleted_order
standard_constraints.expression: deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at >= created_at

db_constraints_policy.enum_validation: DB ENUM types preferred over VARCHAR + CHECK; enum values defined in global enums registry

soft_delete_policy.filter: deleted_at IS NULL applied globally via query builder middleware
soft_delete_policy.cascade: Parent delete soft-deletes children via application cascade, not DB cascade
soft_delete_policy.hard_delete: Prohibited in production; available only via admin CLI with audit log
soft_delete_policy.restore: Set deleted_at = NULL; triggers audit log entry

naming_conventions.date_fields: Always use _at suffix for TIMESTAMPTZ, _date suffix for DATE
naming_conventions.id_fields: Primary key is always id; foreign keys are {entity}_id
